name: B.c.plum-Cot Leather
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash, pit and chop the fruit.
2. Puree in a blender with sugar.
3. Yields 3 1/2 to 4 cups. Prepare a baking dish measuring 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 inches by covering it with plastic wrap and securing with masking tape.
4. Pour the puree evenly over the plastic wrap.
5. Place the baking dish in full sunlight.
6. Allow drying for several hours, depending on the fruit, temperature, and amount of sunlight received.
7. If not dry after the first day, bring indoors overnight and continue drying the next day. It may take up to 24 hours.
8. If weather is unsuitable for sun drying, pre-heat the oven to its lowest setting (150°F).
9. Turn off the oven and place the puree in the middle of the tray.
10. Occasionally reheat to maintain warmth.
11. The leather is ready when it peels easily from the plastic wrap.
12. To store, roll the leather in the plastic wrap, then seal in a bag.
13. At room temperature, the leather will keep for one month. In the refrigerator, it will keep for three months.
27 servings
1 serving strip: 6x1 inch, contains 21 calories; half of a fruit and vegetable choice.
